allies.rationale_text: Constraints help path-dependence become visible by showing how inherited standards, interfaces, and dependencies narrow the real option set.
allies.source_quote: It is a strong fit when habits, standards, interfaces, or prior investments are locking the system into the current route.

allies.rationale_text: Iteration helps path-dependence reveal itself because repeated behavior can expose how the old route keeps reproducing itself despite new intent.
allies.source_quote: Works when a system keeps reproducing old behavior despite new intent.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps path-dependence trace how historical choices, interfaces, and commitments reshape the current route at the system level.
allies.source_quote: Path dependence is strongest when the real driver is inherited architecture, history, or commitment rather than present-day preference alone.

antagonists.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking breaks path-dependence by stripping away inherited route assumptions and rebuilding from the underlying facts.
antagonists.source_quote: First Principles strips away all assumptions and precedents, reducing the problem to its fundamental facts to forge a new, potentially divergent solution.

antagonists.rationale_text: Status-quo-bias can turn a real path-lock diagnosis into an excuse for staying on the inherited route rather than redesigning what can change.
antagonists.source_quote: path-dependence conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when a correctly diagnosed path-lock is used as an excuse to avoid redesign rather than as a genuine structural constraint.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Path-dependence conflicts with status-quo-bias when a correctly diagnosed path-lock is used as an excuse to avoid redesign rather than as a genuine structural constraint.
structured_tensions.source_quote: path-dependence vs status-quo-bias: path-dependence conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when a correctly diagnosed path-lock is used as an excuse to avoid redesign rather than as a genuine structural constraint.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Path-dependence conflicts with confirmation-bias when decision-makers selectively credit evidence that justifies staying on the current path rather than fairly evaluating the case for switching.
structured_tensions.source_quote: path-dependence vs confirmation-bias: path-dependence conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when decision-makers selectively credit evidence that justifies staying on the current path rather than fairly evaluating the case for switching.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on inherited-route allies and two explicit tensions against excuse-making and self-justifying evidence reading.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor path or migration relation sprawl beyond the strongest route-lock allies and explicit tensions.
open_questions: Anchoring is source-real in the broader path-lock neighborhood, but first-principles-thinking, status-quo-bias, and confirmation-bias were the cleaner bounded anchors in the current curated surface.
